notes: From the proton point of view, it runs out of the box but the mesa driver on AMD graphics cards causes gpu hangs so that the whole pc comes to a standstill for it there is only one walkaround at the moment and you have to use the other vulkan driver (amdvlk) but only for this game! Walkaroud: http://ul.to/f/x65rhk Bug report on mesa: https://bugs.freedesktop.org/show_bug.cgi?id=109201 @Alexander

notes: I played for about 4 hours last night. Hosting some private games with another proton user was fine. Added a third user and things got super laggy. Only issue I've noticed aside from the lag is that the mouse is not visible when in the steam overlay (shift-tab) which can be difficult if you're trying to invite players or join someone else via invite. I ended up exiting DRG and just joining my windows friend from the steam friends list. Other than that everything ran super smooth!

notes: Seems to be capped at 60 Hz, and had significant frame drops and freezes while playing. Lowering the resolution and the resolution scale seemed to help a bit, but not completely alleviate the issues. Installing mesa-vulkan-drivers was necessary for the game to start, as without it the game would deliver the error "dx11 feature level 10.0 is required to run the engine".

notes: Works almost perfectly out of the box. I have been playing this game on linux for at least 100 hours now and I encountered only a few problems: Discord integration doesn't work at all. Joining a friends game through steam doesn't work. Accepting an invite or inviting is totally possible though while already being ingame (doing it while the game is not running starts the game but the screen stays black after). On very rare occasions, the game suddenly freezes. This was worse when I had an older GPU installed, but also happened with my current one (once in like 50 hours). Intro not working.
